evidence.scientific_clarity: Directly addresses brief: 3 methods × 50 steps, fill_between CI bands, log-scale y-axis, double-headed arrow with Δ annotation at final step — all brief requirements met.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype='none' set anywhere in script; fonts will be Type 3 (non-editable bitmaps in PDF); font family defaults to DejaVu Sans.
evidence.palette: Uses Seaborn-derived blue/orange/green (#4C72B0, #DD8452, #55A868) — colorblind-safe; linestyle differentiation (solid/dashed/dash-dot) adds robustness beyond color alone.
evidence.layout_density: tight_layout() used and figsize=(6,4) is reasonable; xlim extended by 5 steps (steps[-1]+5) to fit annotation, leaving ~3.5 steps of dead whitespace on the right.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption correctly describes log scale, CI definition, 5 seeds, and the gap arrow; however caption gives no actual numerical Δ value in text — reviewer will note the arrow label is the only quantification.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf is saved (17 KB, non-trivial) but script omits matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42, so text is stored as Type 3 outlines rather than selectable TrueType glyphs.
evidence.file_format: All four required outputs present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (17 KB), figure.png (116 KB), caption.tex (382 B), gen_figure.py (2.2 KB); bonus figure.svg also saved.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains CI bands, log scale, seed count, and references the gap arrow; qualitative claim ('lowest throughout') is made but no explicit numerical Δ value or final-loss magnitude appears in the caption text itself.

overall_strengths: Complete file deliverables with all required outputs present and non-trivial; bonus SVG included.
Colorblind-safe palette combined with distinct linestyles ensures methods are distinguishable without relying on color alone.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype='none') means PDF/SVG text is non-selectable Type 3 — a hard rejection criterion at most journals.
Caption lacks a concrete numerical finding (e.g., the actual Δ value or final loss magnitude), weakening its standalone informativeness for a reviewer.
